analysis_result: 12q gain frequency in MPNST-G2 tumors = 0/8 (0.0%) at both +0.10 and +0.15 arm-level mean log2 thresholds. Highest 12q arm-level mean log2 in MPNST-G2 was +0.084 (M1677), below the +0.10 gain threshold. Reference (Benign_NF-G4) false-positive rate = 0/20 at both thresholds.
denominator_used: N = 8 MPNST_G2 tumors (M1677, M1933, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413, M9450). Reference = 20 Benign_NF-G4 tumors.
assumptions_made: CONUMEE was substituted with a Python methylprep-based log2-ratio pipeline (methylprep only exposes IDAT I/O, not native CONUMEE CNV calls); total probe intensity per probe defined as Grn[A]+Red[A] for Type II and Grn[A]+Grn[B] or Red[A]+Red[B] for Type I depending on Color_Channel; sample-level median normalization of total intensities (dividing by each sample's own median); reference = per-probe median across the 20 Benign_NF-G4 samples; log2 ratio = log2((sample_norm + eps)/(ref_median + eps)); sample-wise median-centering of log2 ratios as baseline correction; 12q defined by hg19 coordinate MAPINFO > 37.8 Mb on chr12 (pericentromere exclusion); arm-level score = mean of log2 ratios across 33,109 12q CpGs; gain threshold +0.10 to +0.15 (primary = 0.10); filtered probes with minimum intensity <100 across samples; no explicit GC/sequence composition correction or segmentation (CONUMEE proper uses GC correction and CBS segmentation, which could shift thresholds); X/Y excluded from baseline; 12q arm coverage 33,109 probes vs 11,318 on 12p.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- Clinical data (MOESM4): 108 samples with Methylation_Group and Sentrix_ID.
- 216 gzipped IDAT files (108 samples x Red+Grn) in GSE207207_idats/.
- GPL21145 MethylationEPIC v1.0 manifest (868,565 rows; 864,935 CpG probes; 845,327 autosomal CpG probes after filter).

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- All MPNST_G2 samples with methylation data: 8 tumors (M1677, M1933, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413, M9450).
- Reference set: all Benign_NF-G4 samples: 20 tumors (NF1059, NF1301, NF1537, NF1560, NF1674, NF1682, NF1693, NF1738, NF1742, NF1847, NF2042, NF2149, NF2162, NF2166, NF2173, NF2249, NF2301, NF2474, NF2978, NF3011).
- All 28 target samples had matched Red+Grn IDAT files on disk.

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Raw IDAT signal per probe read with methylprep.files.IdatDataset for both Red and Green channels.
- Per-probe total intensity computed by design type:
  - Type II (N=705,460): Grn[AddressA] + Red[AddressA]
  - Type I Grn (N=49,076): Grn[AddressA] + Grn[AddressB]
  - Type I Red (N=90,791): Red[AddressA] + Red[AddressB]
- After alignment and low-intensity filtering (min>100 across 28 samples), 842,514 autosomal CpG probes retained.

D. COMPUTATION
1. Each sample's total intensity vector divided by its own median (sample-level normalization).
2. Reference = per-probe median of 20 normalized Benign_NF-G4 samples.
3. log2_ratio[p,s] = log2((norm[p,s]+1e-6) / (ref_median[p]+1e-6)).
4. Each sample's log2-ratio vector centered on its autosomal median (CONUMEE-like baseline correction).
5. 12q defined by chr12 MAPINFO > 37.8 Mb (hg19; 33,109 CpGs). 12p MAPINFO < 34.8 Mb (11,318 CpGs).
6. Per-sample 12q arm score = mean log2 across the 33,109 12q CpGs.
7. Gain called if arm score > threshold (+0.10 primary; +0.15 stringent).

E. RESULT
- 12q arm-level mean log2 for MPNST-G2 samples (sorted):
  M1677  +0.0840
  M9450  +0.0778
  M2377  +0.0629
  M9413  +0.0341
  M4719  +0.0099
  M1933  -0.0013
  M2325  -0.0177
  M2491  -0.0198
- MPNST-G2 mean = +0.029, SD = 0.042. Benign_NF-G4 mean = -0.007, SD = 0.032.
- Threshold +0.10: 0/8 MPNST-G2 (0.0%) called as 12q gain.
- Threshold +0.15: 0/8 MPNST-G2 (0.0%) called as 12q gain.
- Reference false-positive rate: 0/20 (0.0%) at both thresholds.
- Sensitivity check using arm median log2 at +0.10: 1/8 (12.5%) M1677 only; at +0.15: 0/8.

Sanity check: Using the same pipeline across all autosomal arms the MPNST-G2 samples do show arm-level gains on other chromosomes (e.g., M9450: 19p +0.208, 17q +0.178, 7p +0.156; M1933: 7p +0.132, 1q +0.128, 17q +0.116; M2491: 7q +0.117). This confirms the pipeline can detect arm-level gains; 12q simply is not one of the recurrent gains in this cohort.

F. CAVEATS
- CONUMEE proper (R/Bioconductor) uses GC correction, controlled probe filtering, and CBS segmentation; the Python substitute here uses sample median normalization + autosomal-median centering only, which may yield slightly lower arm-level log2 values versus CONUMEE's linear regression-corrected intensities.
- 12q is a large arm; a focal gain restricted to a sub-arm (e.g., 12q13 amplification as in MDM2) would be diluted by averaging across 33,109 CpGs.
- No formal segmentation performed, so arm-level mean is the summary statistic.
- Reference set = Benign_NF-G4 plexiform neurofibromas (n=20); biologically these are not germline normal but near-diploid benign neurofibromas, consistent with the paper's use of benign lesions as CNV baseline.
- Thresholds +0.10 to +0.15 correspond to ~1 extra allele copy diluted by normal cells (e.g., ~0.15 log2 ratio = ~1.1x copy signal, consistent with substantial tumor-cell contribution).
- With this substitute pipeline and these thresholds, 12q gain is not called in any of the 8 MPNST-G2 samples.
